briefdescription.para: unassigned
all-nodes
all-relays
all-friends
all-proxies
all-directed-forwarding-nodes
all-ipt-nodes
all-ipt-border-routers
Key unused.
Any key index.
Device key.
Local device key.
Remote device key.
Any device key.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_mesh_model *model, struct bt_mesh_msg_ctx *ctx, struct net_buf_simple *msg, const struct bt_mesh_send_cb *cb, void *cb_data)
memberdef.name: bt_mesh_model_send

briefdescription.para: Send an Access Layer message.

parameterdescription.para: Mesh (client) Model that the message belongs to.
Message context, includes keys, TTL, etc.
Access Layer payload (the actual message to be sent).
User data to be passed to the callback.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Send a model publication message.

detaileddescription.para: Before calling this function, the user needs to ensure that the model publication message (bt_mesh_model_pub::msg) contains a valid message to be sent. Note that this API is only to be used for non-period publishing. For periodic publishing the app only needs to make sure that bt_mesh_model_pub::msg contains a valid message whenever the bt_mesh_model_pub::update callback is called.

parameterdescription.para: Mesh (client) Model that's publishing the message.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Check if a message is being retransmitted.

detaileddescription.para: Meant to be used inside the bt_mesh_model_pub::update callback.

simplesect.para: true if this is a retransmission, false if this is a first publication.

briefdescription.para: Get the element that a model belongs to.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the element that the given model belongs to.

briefdescription.para: Find a SIG model.

parameterdescription.para: Element to search for the model in.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the Mesh model matching the given parameters, or NULL if no SIG model with the given ID exists in the given element.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_mesh_elem *elem, uint16_t company, uint16_t id)
memberdef.name: bt_mesh_model_find_vnd

briefdescription.para: Find a vendor model.

parameterdescription.para: Element to search for the model in.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the Mesh model matching the given parameters, or NULL if no vendor model with the given ID exists in the given element.

briefdescription.para: Get whether the model is in the primary element of the device.

simplesect.para: true if the model is on the primary element, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_mesh_model *mod, bool vnd, const char *name, const void *data, size_t data_len)
memberdef.name: bt_mesh_model_data_store

briefdescription.para: Immediately store the model's user data in persistent storage.

parameterdescription.para: Name/key of the settings item. Only SETTINGS_MAX_DIR_DEPTH bytes will be used at most.
Model data to store, or NULL to delete any model data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Schedule the model's user data store in persistent storage.

detaileddescription.para: This function triggers the bt_mesh_model_cb::pending_store callback for the corresponding model after delay defined by CONFIG_BT_MESH_STORE_TIMEOUT.
The delay is global for all models. Once scheduled, the callback can not be re-scheduled until previous schedule completes.

briefdescription.para: Let a model extend another.

detaileddescription.para: Mesh models may be extended to reuse their functionality, forming a more complex model. A Mesh model may extend any number of models, in any element. The extensions may also be nested, ie a model that extends another may itself be extended.
A set of models that extend each other form a model extension list.

All models in an extension list share one subscription list per element. The access layer will utilize the combined subscription list of all models in an extension list and element, giving the models extended subscription list capacity.

If CONFIG_BT_MESH_COMP_PAGE_1 is enabled, it is not allowed to call this function before the bt_mesh_model_cb::init callback is called for both models, except if it is called as part of the final callback.

parameterdescription.para: Mesh model that is extending the base model.

briefdescription.para: Let a model correspond to another.

detaileddescription.para: Mesh models may correspond to each other, which means that if one is present, other must be present too. A Mesh model may correspond to any number of models, in any element. All models connected together via correspondence form single Correspondence Group, which has it's unique Correspondence ID. Information about Correspondence is used to construct Composition Data Page 1.

This function must be called on already initialized base_mod. Because this function is designed to be called in corresponding_mod initializer, this means that base_mod shall be initialized before corresponding_mod is.

parameterdescription.para: Mesh model that is corresponding to the base model.
Successfully saved correspondence to the base_mod model.
There is no more space to save this relation.
Composition Data Page 1 is not supported.

briefdescription.para: Check if model is extended by another model.

parameterdescription.para: If model is extended by another model, otherwise false

briefdescription.para: Indicate that the composition data will change on next bootup.

detaileddescription.para: Tell the config server that the composition data is expected to change on the next bootup, and the current composition data should be backed up.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Indicate that the metadata will change on next bootup.

detaileddescription.para: Tell the config server that the models metadata is expected to change on the next bootup, and the current models metadata should be backed up.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Register composition data page 2 of the device.

detaileddescription.para: Register Mesh Profiles information (Ref section 3.12 in Bluetooth SIG Assigned Numbers) for composition data page 2 of the device.

simplesect.para: There must be at least one record present in comp2
Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if a Bluetooth Mesh address is a unicast address.
Check if a Bluetooth Mesh address is a group address.
Check if a Bluetooth Mesh address is a fixed group address.
Check if a Bluetooth Mesh address is a virtual address.
Check if a Bluetooth Mesh address is an RFU address.
Check if a Bluetooth Mesh key is a device key.
Maximum size of an access message segment (in octets).
Maximum payload size of an unsegmented access message (in octets).
Maximum number of segments supported for incoming messages.
Maximum number of segments supported for outgoing messages.
Maximum possible payload size of an outgoing access message (in octets).
Maximum possible payload size of an incoming access message (in octets).
Helper to define a mesh element within an array.

detaileddescription.para: In case the element has no SIG or Vendor models the helper macro BT_MESH_MODEL_NONE can be given instead.

briefdescription.para: Macro for encoding a 1-byte opcode (used by Bluetooth SIG defined models).
Macro for encoding a 2-byte opcode (used by Bluetooth SIG defined models).
Macro for encoding a 3-byte opcode (vendor-specific message).
Macro for encoding exact message length for fixed-length messages.
Macro for encoding minimum message length for variable-length messages.
End of the opcode list.

detaileddescription.para: Must always be present.

briefdescription.para: Helper to define an empty opcode list.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

briefdescription.para: Helper to define an empty model array.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

briefdescription.para: Composition data SIG model entry with callback functions with specific number of keys & groups.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Number of keys that can be bound to the model. Shall not exceed CONFIG_BT_MESH_MODEL_KEY_COUNT.
Number of addresses that the model can be subscribed to. Shall not exceed CONFIG_BT_MESH_MODEL_GROUP_COUNT.
Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Composition data vendor model entry with callback functions with specific number of keys & groups.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Number of keys that can be bound to the model. Shall not exceed CONFIG_BT_MESH_MODEL_KEY_COUNT.
Number of addresses that the model can be subscribed to. Shall not exceed CONFIG_BT_MESH_MODEL_GROUP_COUNT.
Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Composition data SIG model entry with callback functions.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Composition data SIG model entry with callback functions and metadata.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.
Metadata structure. Used if CONFIG_BT_MESH_LARGE_COMP_DATA_SRV is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Composition data vendor model entry with callback functions.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Composition data vendor model entry with callback functions and metadata.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

parameterdescription.para: Callback structure, or NULL to keep no callbacks.
Metadata structure. Used if CONFIG_BT_MESH_LARGE_COMP_DATA_SRV is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Composition data SIG model entry.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

briefdescription.para: Composition data vendor model entry.

detaileddescription.para: This macro uses compound literal feature of C99 standard and thus is available only from C, not C++.

briefdescription.para: Encode transmission count & interval steps.

parameterdescription.para: Number of retransmissions (first transmission is excluded).
Interval steps in milliseconds. Must be greater than 0, less than or equal to 320, and a multiple of 10.

simplesect.para: Mesh transmit value that can be used e.g. for the default values of the configuration model data.

briefdescription.para: Decode transmit count from a transmit value.

simplesect.para: Transmission count (actual transmissions is N + 1).

briefdescription.para: Decode transmit interval from a transmit value.

simplesect.para: Transmission interval in milliseconds.

briefdescription.para: Encode Publish Retransmit count & interval steps.

parameterdescription.para: Number of retransmissions (first transmission is excluded).
Interval steps in milliseconds. Must be greater than 0 and a multiple of 50.

simplesect.para: Mesh transmit value that can be used e.g. for the default values of the configuration model data.

briefdescription.para: Decode Publish Retransmit count from a given value.

parameterdescription.para: Encoded Publish Retransmit count & interval value.

simplesect.para: Retransmission count (actual transmissions is N + 1).

briefdescription.para: Decode Publish Retransmit interval from a given value.

parameterdescription.para: Encoded Publish Retransmit count & interval value.

simplesect.para: Transmission interval in milliseconds.

briefdescription.para: Get total number of messages within one publication interval including initial publication.

simplesect.para: total number of messages.

briefdescription.para: Get message number within one publication interval.

detaileddescription.para: Meant to be used inside bt_mesh_model_pub::update.

simplesect.para: message number starting from 1.

briefdescription.para: Define a model publication context.

parameterdescription.para: Variable name given to the context.
Optional message update callback (may be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Initialize a Models Metadata entry structure in a list.

parameterdescription.para: ID of the Models Metadata entry.
Pointer to a contiguous memory that contains the metadata.

briefdescription.para: Helper to define an empty Models metadata array.
End of the Models Metadata list.

detaileddescription.para: Must always be present.

briefdescription.para: Special TTL value to request using configured default TTL.
Maximum allowed TTL value.
Access layer.
